{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 -->
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = '''Styled''' {{ill|Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏|lt=Tachibana|lt_ja=橘}}-{{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}<br />{{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|lt=Kumano|lt_ja=熊野}}-{{ill|Atai|ja|直}}<br />later Kumano-{{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}
|家祖 = {{ill|Ōato-no-Sukune|ja|大阿刀足尼}}
|種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenjin|ja|天神}})
|出身地 = {{ill|Ōyamato Province|ja|大和国|lt=Ōyamato Province|lt_ja=大倭国}}
|根拠地 = Kumano Province (later {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}})
|人物 = See [[#People|People]]
|支流 = {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}}<br />{{ill|Masago clan|ja|真砂氏}}<br />{{ill|Otonashi clan|ja|音無氏}}, etc.
}}
'''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''kumano no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''kumano kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled [[#Territory|Kumano Province]] (later the southern part of {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}).

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry ===
* The "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' transmits that {{ill|Ōato-no-Sukune|ja|大阿刀足尼}}, 5th-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, became Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the era of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.
* The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' "Yamashiro (Shinbetsu, Tenjin)" states that '''Kumano-no-Muraji'''<ref>Since Inahi, son of Ōato-no-Sukune (''ōato no sukune''), the first kuni no miyatsuko, was granted the kabane of "Atai" (''atae''), the name was originally "Kumano-no-Atai." Among his descendants was Iotae, who was appointed Kumano Hongū negi, and when Iotae's grandson Chō held the position, the main family was granted the kabane of Muraji (''muraji'').</ref> was the descendant of Ajinikita-no-Mikoto, grandson of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto.

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏}}''' (''kumano-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}}). In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, they produced {{ill|Kumano Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜}}.

According to the "Kumano-shi Wada Keizu," after {{ill|Kumano Province|ja|熊野国}} was incorporated into {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, they held the positions of {{ill|Muro District|ja|牟婁郡}} {{ill|Dairyō|ja|大領}} and {{ill|Kumano Hongū|ja|熊野本宮}} {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, and venerated the main shrine for generations. Further, according to the "Kumano-shi Wada Keizu," in the era of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}}, {{ill|Tachibana Hirokata|ja|橘広方|lt=Kumano Hirokata (Tachibana Hirokata)|lt_ja=熊野広方（橘広方）}}, son of Kumano-no-Muraji Hirotsugu and Muro District Dairyō, changed his surname to the '''{{ill|Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏|lt=Tachibana surname|lt_ja=橘姓}}''' (possibly becoming the {{ill|yūshi|ja|猶子}} of {{ill|Tachibana Yoshitane|ja|橘良殖}}). Hirotsugu's daughter married {{ill|Suzuki Yoshiuji|ja|鈴木良氏}} and became the mother of Suzuki Shigeuji. Three generations after Hirokata, Tachibana Yoshifuyu (Wada Yoshifuyu) styled himself '''Wada-Shōji'''.

From that time, the kuni no miyatsuko family styled itself Wada-Shōji (Wada no Kuni no Miyatsuko) and became the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Wada clan|lt_ja=和田氏}}. During the era of {{ill|shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, it was pushed aside by the {{ill|Kumano Bettō|ja|熊野別当}} forces, which were the leading shamanic-priestly forces of the Kumano Sanzan backed by the center, but the descendants retained power as {{ill|dogō|ja|土豪}} throughout {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and elsewhere.

== Base ==
=== Territory ===
The territory of the kuni no miyatsuko was the region then called '''Kumano Province''', corresponding to the later {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}} '''{{ill|Muro District|ja|牟婁郡}}''', equivalent to the southern part of present-day {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}} and the southern part of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. To the east it bordered the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造}}; to the northwest, the {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}}.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
Possibly '''{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}}''', a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} in {{ill|Muro District|ja|牟婁郡}} of {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}. It holds {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} rank.

=== Related Shrines ===
*{{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} (''kumano hayatama-taisha'')
*:A Shikinai-sha enshrined in {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮市|lt=Shingū City|lt_ja=新宮市}}.

=== Tombs ===
*'''{{ill|Shimosato Kofun|ja|下里古墳}}''' (''shimosato-kofun'')
*:A {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} with a total length of approximately 40 meters, constructed from the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} to the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Said to be the only keyhole-shaped kofun in the southern part of the Kii Peninsula.

== Descendants ==
* In the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', it is recorded that the {{ill|uneme|ja|采女}} of Muro District, {{ill|Kumano-no-Atai Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|lt=Kumano-no-Atai Hirohama|lt_ja=熊野直広浜}} (''hirohama''), served from the era of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to the era of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|孝謙天皇|lt=Empress Shōtoku|lt_ja=称徳天皇}} (4 reigns in total) (died in {{ill|Jingo-keiun 3|ja|769年|lt=Jingo-keiun 3|lt_ja=神護景雲3年}}, {{ill|Jū-shi-i-ge|ja|従四位下}}).
* In the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the branch family Masago-Shōji also prospered, being called the "{{ill|chōja|ja|長者|lt=chōja|lt_ja=長者}} of Masago." In the {{ill|Princess Kiyo|ja|清姫}} legend, the heroine is said to be the daughter of Masago-no-Shōji Kiyotsugu (in older traditions, the wife or widow of Kiyotsugu). There is also a theory that the {{ill|Masago clan|ja|真砂氏}} (Takiri Ōji-sha estate) was one of the "{{ill|Kumano Hachi-Shōji|ja|熊野八庄司}}" appearing in the ''{{ill|Taiheiki|ja|太平記}}'' and other works.
* There is also a theory that the {{ill|Kawachi Wada clan|ja|河内和田氏}} of the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代_(日本)|lt=Nanboku-chō period|lt_ja=南北朝時代}} (related to the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏}}) are descendants of the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko{{Sfn|Ōta Akira|1935|pp=2144&ndash;2145}}.

== Footnotes ==
<!--===注釈===-->
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Notelist}}
<!--===出典===-->
{{Reflist}}

== References ==
* {{オープンアクセス}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Ōta Akira|authorlink=太田亮|others={{ill|Ueda Kazutoshi|ja|上田萬年}} and {{ill|Mikami Sanji|ja|三上参次}}, eds.|chapter=Kumano  Kumanu  Yuya|pages=2142-2152|volume=2|date=1935|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/165}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ncid=BN05000207|oclc=673726070|ref=harv}}

==See also==
{{Col|
* {{ill|Kumano Province|ja|熊野国}}
* {{ill|Muro District|ja|牟婁郡}}
* {{ill|Nanki|ja|南紀}}
|
* {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ}}
* {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏}}
* {{ill|Southern Court|ja|南朝 (日本)|lt=Southern Court|lt_ja=南朝}}
}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:くまののくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:三重県の歴史]]
[[Category:和歌山県の歴史]]
[[Category:楠木氏|+]]
[[Category:紀伊国]]
[[Category:紀伊国の氏族]]
[[Category:物部氏|-くまののくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:熊野国造]]
